title: Chapter 35
text: Chapter Thirty - Two L ara had spent countless nights huddled beside Tabitha in one drafty shelter or another, desperate for warmth. They suffered through blistering heat and freezing rain, bodies ravaged by hunger, weariness, and pain. Together, they’d survived the worst Cheyenne could throw at them for more years than should have been possible . Those years, those hardships, had not prepared Lara for her first glimpse of her old home after months away . The morning sun had not yet crested the horizon, leaving the sky a dull, dreary gray. The hobbled shacks were dark shapes against that sky, grave markers for the not-yet dead. Even the people who weren’t sick or starving here were dying. She’d never forget the despair and hopelessness that had gripped her during her time in the slums. Only Tabitha had kept Lara from succumbing to it…but she’d been claimed by this place , too . Another body for the heap . Newton and handful of men had broken off to wait on the outskirts of the slums while Lara guided Cooper and his team along the narrow dirt pathways between the shacks. Before she led them to the center of town, to the one place everyone had to go — the water pump — they had a stop to make . The solid metal of the ring brushed over Lara’s skin beneath her shirt, a reminder of what she stood to lose — everything . She and Ronin hadn’t said goodbye. All they needed to say had been communicated the night before, through their words and their bodies . She adjusted her hood and dropped her hand to the pistol on her thigh, focusing on the feel of its grip rather than the stench of rot and human waste. People were just beginning to emerge from their homes. Even in the dim light, Lara noted the recognition in some of their eyes. What did they see? The thin, desperate waif she’d been, or the healthy, well-fed woman she’d become ? Or, did some of them see a walking corpse ? Lara forced her eyes ahead as she neared her destination. She had a clear mission, and it would require her full attention. Gary and Kate were respected and well-liked by many, and they’d always been kind to Lara; if she could convince them to join her, it’d be a damn good start . Her steps faltered as their shack came into view . “No,” she breathed . Warlord’s symbol was on their door, red paint dripping like blood . She rushed across the remaining distance and fumbled to shove the door aside . The foul odor of old blood assaulted her nostrils when she entered. Behind her, the bootsteps of her escort were frantic as they hurried to catch up. A faint light came from the small, secondary room. A man stepped in front of it. His dark form approached, the steel of a blade flashing in his hand. She sucked in a sharp breath . “Lara?” He stopped abruptly . As her eyes adjusted, she realized the figure was Gary. “What happened ?” He moved past her, toward the entryway. She gestured for the soldiers to hang back before Gary slid the door into place . Gary turned to her. He was haggard, with dark circles beneath his eyes. His gaze shimmered with sorrow, but there was something deeper — something fiery — within it . “They’re gone,” he grated . Lara’s heart stuttered. “ Maggie ?” Dropping his eyes, he shook his head . Sweet-faced Maggie, who always offered Lara a kiss and a gap-toothed smile? The only source of light and joy in Cheyenne, the only innocent life who hadn’t yet been touched by hardship ? “Kate, too?” Lara’s throat was raw . Gary ran a shaky hand over his face. “I don’t…I don’t know. She’s… not well .” “She’s alive ?” He nodded . Lara hesitated, silenced by the horror of her next question. “ The … baby ?” “They found out.” He squeezed his eyes shut, tears falling from their corners. “Somehow, they found out. We were so careful, Lara, but those fuckers still found out. He wanted to make an example of us, and he …” “You don’t have to tell me,” Lara said, stepping closer. She stopped when he shook his head . “They killed my little girl. My beautiful little girl.” He turned away and leaned against the wall, body quaking. “They held us down and forced us to watch. Forced us to listen to Maggie crying for help, to her screams , my God her screams …and when she wasn’t moving anymore … “There was so much blood. But…but he wasn’t done. They dragged Kate away from Maggie, and…and they beat her.” Gary slammed his fists against the wall. The shack rattled. “Hit her again, and again, until she bled. Until she…lost the baby .” Breaths quick and shallow, Lara glanced down at her hands. She recalled the pain of Warlord’s fists all too well. Her stomach clenched, threatening to empty itself. How much suffering would be forced upon these people ? No more. We’ll rid the world of this monster . “Is…is Kate here , Gary ?” He nodded again, wiped at his face, and led her into the back room . Kate lay upon the bed, pale and sunken-eyed, her hair damp with sweat. Lara knelt beside her and lifted Kate’s hand. It was cold and limp, as though the life had been sapped from her . “ Kate ?” The woman turned her head. Her eyes were unfocused, staring through Lara. She blinked, and something dawned in her gaze; her face crumpled and she shook with sobs. “My babies …” "Shh, I know." Lara pressed Kate's hand to her cheek. "We will stop him ." "How?" Gary demanded . "There are people here," Lara replied, lowering Kate's hand but not releasing it, "who plan to end Warlord. To take this city back. To help us ." Kate stared up at Lara with watery eyes, silent as Gary knelt beside her. Lara met his gaze. He scrutinized her, as though seeing her for the first time, noting the changes . “People said…you were either dead, or with one of them .” “I’m with a bot, but he’s not one of them,” she said. “He took care of me. Protected me .” Gary's expression darkened. "He forced — " "No! No. He didn't force me. He's never forced me. I love him, Gary, and as crazy as it sounds, he loves me. We’ve both suffered because of Warlord. Without Ronin, I’d be dead.” Lara’s glanced at Kate. “I can’t tell you everything now, but there are people here — humans and bots, both — who are going to put an end to all this. There’s a place not far away, underground, where they’ve thrived. They have gardens, animals, children in school, housing. An army. They’re here to help, but they need our help , too .” “How?” Kate asked. Her hold on Lara’s hand tightened . “ How ?” “Another group’s heading for the Bot District right now. They might even be there already. They’re going to take over the hospital and make a distraction. I’m here to gather up anyone who’s willing to fight. Anyone willing to stand up to Warlord .” “I will,” Gary said. “I want him torn apart .” “Can you help me with the others? I know…I know there doesn’t seem to be much hope…but we can make a better life for all of us .” He turned toward Kate. “Anything. She’s all I have left .” Kate released Lara’s hand to take Gary’s, closing her eyes as he leaned down and kissed her forehead . “I’ll be back,” Lara said . She nudged the front door aside and stepped out. The soldiers had dispersed, but she quickly picked out their shadowy forms in the gloom. Lara waved one of them over . The soldier tugged down the scarf from his mouth as he approached. He held the butt of his rifle against his shoulder. “What’s wrong ?” “There’s a woman inside who needs to be taken to the forward camp. I don’t think she can make it on her own . “Who’s he?” Gary asked. Lara looked over her shoulder to see him in the doorway . “Private Peterson,” the soldier replied. “Where is she ?” “In the back room.” Lara pointed beyond Gary. “Her name’s Kate .” Peterson nodded, leaning over to peer inside the shack. “All right. We’ll make sure to bring her when we move out .” “Bring her where?” Gary asked . “Some of the soldiers are going to escort the children, elderly, and anyone too sick to fight to a safe place before anything starts. After I do what I need to here, I’ll be heading there, too.” She took gentle hold of his arm. “They’ll take care of her. I promise .” Gary stared at her for a long while, finally nodding. “I trust you , Lara .” “Help me convince the others. The more people stand against Warlord, the better. We can’t allow him to do this anymore .” “Let me talk to her. Make sure she knows what’s happening .” “We’ll be at the water pump .” Lara met Peterson’s gaze as Gary went inside . “She’ll be in good hands,” the soldier said . “I know. Thank you .” The soldiers followed Lara as she wound through the slums, knocking on doors and calling to people through tent flaps. She roused some from their sleep, but many were already awake, preparing for the long, unforgiving day ahead . She told all of them to gather at the water pump. More than one person swore at her, and a few — especially as the light strengthened and the bits of red hair beneath her hood became clear — called her a traitor. Lara didn’t let it faze her; this was their one and only chance, and she’d see it through . Gary caught up with her before long, and people seemed more willing to listen to him. His loss was recent, but had been witnessed by many, and everyone had suffered somehow at the hands of the gearheads. They were everywhere — men and women missing fingers, or hands, or legs. Victims of justice . More homes than she remembered bore Warlord’s symbol on their doors . When they finally reached the common area around the water pump, a crowd had gathered, speaking to each other in hushed voices. Tension thickened the air . She found Newton and the other soldiers on the fringes of the crowd. He was wrapped in cloth from head to toe, his casing still bare . “Any sign of gearheads?” Lara asked . “No. Everything is, relatively, quiet .” “Won’t be for much longer,” Cooper said, gaze shifting to the northeast. “Best get on with this, so we can move when the time is right .” Lara brushed her sweat-dampened palms over her skirt and approached the crowd . We will make this work. We have to . “What’s this all about?” an older man demanded. Lara recognized his scowling face; Steve had been a fieldworker when she was young, one of the few humans allowed to toil under the watch of the gearheads, until a cut on his leg had become infected. He’d kept the leg, but never overcame his limp. “I could be out pickin’ already. We’re losin’ good time !” His voice carried over the other conversation, and attention swung toward Lara . “Some of us ain’t spreading our legs to bang bots for food.” Scarlet sneered, glaring at Lara with obvious envy. They were nearly the same age, but had never been close; Tabitha was Lara’s only real friend . Clamping her mouth shut, Lara closed her eyes. She wouldn’t allow herself to give in to anger. She needed these people on her side, on their own side. When she opened her eyes again, she ran her gaze over every person she could see, meeting their eyes one-by-one. They were scared, hungry, uncertain. The only difference between Lara and these people was that she’d learned to hope for something better . “I’m not going to lie to you. I left here with a bot. A dustwalker named Ronin. I did it because I was hungry, yeah, but more because I wanted to find my sister .” “Another bot-fucking whore.” Scarlet spat on the ground at Lara’s feet . “That’s enough!” Gary stepped forward. At the edges of her vision, Lara saw Cooper and his men closing in , too . “You gonna stand here and defend this filth?” Steve demanded . “Filth? Lara sacrificed to help my family when no one else would. She had nothing , and still gave to us. She traded good salvage for scraps of food, and we took it for granted. I’ll always regret that. But I’m standing beside her now…like I should’ve, all along .” “The bots she left with killed your children!” someone shouted . Gary’s face tightened, and he clenched his fists at his sides. “No. Warlord killed my children, and Lara is here to end him .” Humorless laughter spread through parts of the crowd . “She ain’t ending anything !” “Not by myself,” Lara replied . “You can’t expect us to fight him !” “Why? Why can’t we fight him? Because he’s put fear in our hearts?” Lara nodded to Cooper, and he stepped forward with his soldiers. They stood out despite the tattered coats and cloaks over their uniforms. “These people came to stand beside us, to join our fight against Warlord .” “Who the hell are they?” Steve asked . “Soldiers,” Lara said, turning back toward the crowd. “And this is only a handful of them .” “Why would they help us ?” “I asked them to .” Everyone was silent. They stared at Lara, stared at the soldiers. Conflict was clear on so many of their faces . “Warlord killed my sister and dismantled the bot who was caring for her because they broke his rules. Me and Ronin left before he could do the same to us. We risked the Dust to be together. Call me whatever you want, but Ronin is as human as any of us, and he’s mine . He may be built different, but he thinks. He feels . “And Warlord chased us down. Through a fucking dust storm. He chased us down, deactivated Ronin, and beat me near to death. He left me in the dirt to die, just so I’d suffer a little longer. That’s all he’s ever done. Our pain amuses him. Haven’t we all suffered enough ?” Murmured voices spread through the crowd. People shifted uncomfortably on their feet, staring at mutilated limbs, at neighbors who’d lost loved ones . “Newton, the bot who offered us shelter during the storm, is the only reason I’m standing here now .” She turned and met Newton’s gaze. The plates over his eyes shifted subtly, and Lara nodded at him . After a moment’s hesitation, he stepped forward, pulling down his hood and removing the cloth from his lower face. The people gasped, their quiet conversations fueled with new energy . “He reactivated Ronin, and took us to a place where we could find help. These people—” Lara gestured at the soldiers “—saved us. They fed me, tended our wounds, gave us shelter. And now, they’re here to help .” She pointed in the direction of the wall, the top of which was visible over the shacks. “As we speak, there’s another team going in there. They’re going to start the fight, but it’s up to us to rally together to finish it. Together, we will march into the Market, and Newton will convince the bots to join us .” The faces in the crowd were a variety of conflicting emotions — hope, fear, doubt, determination . “We can only do it if we’re all together. As one, we can defeat him. We can take back this city and live .” “What the hell is going on here?” a familiar voice called . Lara clenched her jaw as Devon pushed his way to the front of the crowd. She hadn’t spoken to him since the day Ronin took her to the Bot District . He looked the same as she remembered, but her experience had altered her perspective. Beneath the dirt smudges on his cheeks and the overly-large rags over his clothes, he was a well-built man with a filled-out face and a healthy color to his skin. A man who always had plenty of food. A man who always avoided the gearheads despite the attention his wealth should have brought . “We’re taking down Warlord,” she said . Devon’s stride faltered and his eyes widened before he burst into laughter. He doubled over, hand on his stomach, guffawing. Lara — and the entire crowd — stood silent as he wandered closer to her, eyes filled with mirth . “What can you do? Besides fucking bots, I mean.” He stroked his crotch. “You didn’t need to run off with one of them if you needed it so badly .” “We don’t have time for this shit, Devon.” She shifted her attention to the others. “If you want to be free of Warlord, stand with us. Anyone too weak to fight will be taken to a safe location where — ” “You’re fucking serious.” Devon furrowed his brow and turned to the crowd. “You believe this? Follow this bot-banger, and you know what’ll happen. You’ll all get killed .” “Those men are soldiers. Look at their guns,” someone said . “Warlord’s got guns, too,” Devon replied. “And even without them, one of his bots could tear any of us apart with its bare hands .” “What other choice do we have?” another person demanded . “Go home and keep living !” “Easy for you to say.” Steve glared at Devon. “How is it you got so much, boy? How is it they ain’t come knocking on your door yet ?” Devon shrugged. “Not my fault if you aren’t resourceful enough to get what you need without digging through trash .” Lara’s breath caught in her throat. With his words, everything fell into place. No one got by without trading in this world. For most of them, that meant scavenging, scraping together meager crop harvests, or putting their unique talents to use — shoemaking or tailoring or making pottery. She’d never seen Devon work. Never saw him hide when the gearheads came around. And they always seemed to know exactly who to look for, who to punish . “It was you ,” she said . He looked over his shoulder at her, frowning . “ What ?” Lara stepped forward, and, as he turned to face her, swung her arm. Her fist connected with his mouth, and his lips mashed against his teeth. The pain in her hand was distant, muted by her rage. His head snapped to the side and he tumbled into the dirt. A small cloud of dust rose around him . “It was you all along, you son of a bitch !” Devon pushed himself up on hands and knees, head down, and spat blood. Glancing up, he met her eyes. He held her gaze as he slowly stood, blood trickling down his chin from his split lips. His hands fell to his sides. “Best watch what you say, bot slut .” “Or what? You going to tell the gearheads, like you told them about Kate ?” A collective gasp washed over the crowd . “What?” Gary asked, moving closer . “Devon is Warlord’s rat. How else would he live so easy, while they always know who breaks the rules ?” “Is it true?” a young woman asked. Her face was pale beneath a layer of grim, and the two smallest fingers of her right hand were gone. Lara recognized her as one of Devon’s past lovers . “Are you going to believe this bitch? This fucking traitor ?” “Only one traitor here,” Steve said, crossing his arms over his chest, his steely glare fixed on Devon . Gary growled. The sound grew into a bellow of rage as he charged Devon, tackling the man to the ground. They rolled, Gary punching wildly. Devon forced a boot between them, kicking Gary backward, and scrambled to his feet. He drew a knife from his belt and turned his wild-eyed gaze to Lara . “You fucking bitch !” Before he advanced a single step, the soldiers placed themselves in front of Lara, rifles raised . “Drop your weapon,” Cooper commanded . “Don’t shoot him,” she said. “He’s not worth throwing our chance away .” Devon halted, staring at the barrel of Cooper’s gun. Steve lunged forward, grasping Devon’s arm with both hands and twisting, forcing him to drop the knife . “You’re all going to pay for this!” Devon pulled away from Steve’s hold. “Do you think you can defeat Warlord?” He laughed, exposing his crimson-stained teeth . Stooping, Gary picked up the knife, not looking away from Devon . “Stay the fuck away from me.” Devon retreated toward the crowd. “You’ll all be dead before — ” From all sides, the crowd took hold of Devon, grabbing fistfuls of his clothes and hair, clamping their hands on his arms and legs . “What the fuck?” he shouted. “Let go of me !” Their hold on him tightened as he thrashed, and they lifted him off his feet, forcing his arms and legs to the sides. Gary walked forward slowly. “You killed them .” “Get the fuck a—” Devon’s words were cut off by his cry of pain as Gary thrust the knife into his gut. “God, you fucking — ” The crowd was eerily silent. Gary pulled the knife free and plunged it in again, and again, and again, into Devon’s abdomen and chest, covering his hand and forearm in blood . “You stole their lives, you bastard. Took them from me, took them from me !” Devon’s eyes bulged and the tendons on his neck stood out. He lifted his bobbing head to look Gary in the eye . “I hope there’s a hell, so you can fucking burn in it,” Gary said, and slashed the knife across Devon’s throat. Blood poured from the gaping wound to pool on the dirt below . Everyone watched as Gary stepped back and Devon’s lifeless body was tossed, face-first, onto the ground. Gary’s eyes gleamed. His arm and chest were splattered with blood, shoulders rising and falling with his ragged breaths. The kind, sweet man Lara had always known was gone; if she’d done something sooner, could she have saved Gary from this ? “I’ll fight,” he said. “To the death. I want that fucking bot ended .” “Who else?” Lara asked, looking over the grim faces . “I will.” Steve took a single step forward . “ Me , too .” “ And me .” One-by-one, the crowd agreed . “How can you be sure we’ll win?” Scarlet asked. Whatever fire had driven her to insult Lara earlier had been replaced by pale-faced uncertainty . “I’m not. But it’s worth — ” Rapid, distant gunfire echoed through the sky. Lara, along with most of the crowd, looked northeast. In that moment, she realized what she’d always known but refused to acknowledge — Ronin wasn’t invincible, and she’d almost lost him too many times already . Please. Please let him be okay . “We must go, Lara.” Newton touched her arm as he came up beside her . Swallowing thickly, she nodded to him. “The fight just started,” she called to the crowd, “but the choice is yours. Will you battle for your freedom, or will you die in the dirt under Warlord’s boot ?”
